source_pr.title: disttask: fix flaky multi task test
source_pr.body:

test.name: disttask: fix flaky multi task test

symptoms: Brittle multi-task assertion: require task[0]=Reverted and task[1..]=Succeed (which task fails can be nondeterministic under concurrency)
failure_signature: Brittle multi-task assertion expects a specific task index to be reverted/succeeded in a concurrent run
root_cause_explanation: This case is flaky because the multi-task test assumed a deterministic mapping between task index and final state (e.g., `tasks[0]` must be reverted and `tasks[1..]` must succeed). Under concurrent scheduling, the exact task that hits the injected failure / revert path can vary, so asserting per-index outcomes is brittle and can fail intermittently. The fix makes the assertion order-independent by checking only that exactly one task ends up reverted, regardless of which task index it is.
fix_pattern: In concurrent multi-task tests, avoid asserting exact per-index task outcomes; instead assert an invariant (e.g., exactly one task is reverted/failed) to make the test resilient to nondeterministic scheduling.

evidence: evidence: disttask: fix flaky multi task test
